refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies implementation. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the functional capability without prescribing how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single obligation: achieving the energy efficiency target defined by the customer.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through measurement that the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target over the agreed evaluation period.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish predictive maintenance to ensure energy-efficient operation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational policies may influence control parameters
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and control, which can be modularized independently from other system functions, supporting decoupling. Potential coupling arises if operational policies tightly constrain control logic, but this can be managed through interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and reliable energy measurement is feasible within system constraints
Operational policies allow for dynamic control adjustments
Energy efficiency targets are achievable through control and monitoring alone
Risk that measurement errors or delays degrade control effectiveness
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for ensuring measurement accuracy and data integrity to support the control DP, enhancing robustness and independence.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Contains physical energy consumption but lacks direct control and monitoring logic, causing moderate diffusion.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy monitoring; would cause high diffusion.
Safety may use energy data but is not responsible for monitoring/control; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, as it centralizes energy management functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may require careful interface design.
Integration with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces must ensure timely and reliable data exchange to maintain real-time control.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing the necessary monitoring and control mechanisms to meet the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target acceptance criterion.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on the Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause diffusion if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating good containment of the DP. Other modules like Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services have higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data and energy consumption metrics collected via the NGHE Solution module interfaces, enabling measurement against the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting real-time data exchange and control.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define and formalize the interface with the Vehicle module to ensure reliable and timely raw energy data delivery, minimizing diffusion risk.
Establish robust integration protocols with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to guarantee real-time performance and data integrity for feedback control.
Document contingency plans for handling data delays or failures from external modules to maintain control effectiveness and safety compliance.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to dependency on the Vehicle module and integration with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces necessitate revisions to ensure robust and safe operation. Explicit interface definitions, integration protocols, and contingency plans must be formalized to mitigate diffusion risks and maintain control effectiveness and safety compliance before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly define and formalize interfaces and integration protocols with external modules (Vehicle, Telemetry, Network Link) and document contingency plans to reduce diffusion risks and ensure safety-critical performance.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation module for the DP.
Interfaces with Vehicle, Telemetry, and Network Link modules are critical for real-time data and control exchange.
open_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause diffusion if interfaces are not well defined.
Potential data delays or failures from external modules impacting control effectiveness and safety.
evidence_needed: Formal interface specifications with Vehicle, Telemetry, and Network Link modules.
Integration protocol documentation ensuring real-time performance and data integrity.
Contingency plans for handling data delays or failures.
